pages.rationale: This is the cover page of the document and contains no content suitable for question generation.
This page contains the table of contents and provides no factual or conceptual material for questions.
This is a continuation of the table of contents and is not suitable for question generation.
This page is an introductory note and advertisement for the document's features and smart quiz, containing no UPSC-relevant facts.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Extremely dense page covering the constitutional basis of Emergency (Articles 352-360), types of emergencies, proclamation procedures, and parliamentary approval requirements.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.subtopic: Constitutional Amendments and Assent to State Bills
pages.rationale: High-value content regarding the 38th, 39th, 42nd, and 44th Amendments, plus a significant Supreme Court judgment on the Presidential Reference regarding assent to bills.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.subtopic: Constitutional Provisions Regarding Assent to State Bills
pages.rationale: Detailed breakdown of Articles 200 and 201, including the Governor's and President's responsibilities and the legal nuances of the writ of mandamus.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.subtopic: Governor's Powers and Office of Vice President
pages.rationale: Covers Governor's discretion, Article 361 immunity, and the election procedure for the Vice-President, including the 39th Amendment's impact on election disputes.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.subtopic: Vice-President and Speaker of Lok Sabha
pages.rationale: Contains constitutional provisions for the Vice-President (Articles 64-68) and the Speaker of Lok Sabha (Article 93-94), including qualifications and removal processes.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.subtopic: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha
pages.rationale: Discusses the core duties of the Speaker and the historical background and constitutional role of the Deputy Speaker under Articles 94 and 95.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Dense factual content on the 10th Schedule, 52nd Amendment, judicial pronouncements (Kihoto Hollohan, etc.), and constitutional provisions for disqualification (Articles 102 and 191).

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Covers RPA 1951 Section 8, the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill 2025 regarding removal of ministers, and the origins and authority of Parliamentary Committees.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: High density of information on the Estimates Committee, Consultative Committees, CCS, ACC, and the newly introduced Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI).

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Covers the Model Youth Gram Sabha (MYGS), Official Language provisions (Part XVII), and the Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU), along with Money Bill basics.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Very dense page explaining Finance Bills vs. Money Bills, Private Member Bills, Adjournment Sine Die, and Zero Hour with specific procedural details.

pages.topic: Functioning of Parliament, State Legislature and Local Government
pages.rationale: Explains types of questions (Starred, Unstarred, Short Notice) and the National e-Vidhan Application (NeVA). Moderate factual density.
Detailed information on the Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act 2013, including composition, eligibility, and tenure of the Lokpal.
Covers disqualifications for Lokpal members, the composition of the Selection Committee, and the specific functions of the Inquiry and Prosecution wings.

pages.subtopic: Lokpal and Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI)
pages.rationale: This page contains high-density factual information regarding the jurisdiction of Lokpal and the structural/legal framework of the CBI, including its genesis, appointment process, and specific operations.

pages.subtopic: CBI Consent and Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)
pages.rationale: Covers critical legal aspects of state consent for CBI and comprehensive constitutional details of UPSC including composition, tenure, and independence safeguards.
Highly dense page covering NCRB functions, suicide statistics, prison governance (State List), the Model Prisons Act 2023, and landmark judicial pronouncements on undertrials.
Detailed information on the global accreditation process (GANHRI/Paris Principles) and the internal composition/appointment committee of the NHRC.
Focuses on the civil court powers and specific functions of the NHRC, along with a brief mention of World Accreditation Day.

pages.subtopic: NSCSTI, Capacity Building Commission, and iGOT Karmayogi
pages.rationale: Contains specific details on Mission Karmayogi components, the CBC's role, and the new NSCSTI 2.0 framework for training institutes.

pages.subtopic: CAG and Attorney General of India
pages.rationale: Provides essential constitutional facts, tenure, removal procedures, and rights/limitations for both the CAG and the Attorney General.
Details the transition from MCI to NMC, the structure of autonomous boards under NMC, and the constitutional mandate of the NCSC.
Extremely dense page covering Article 22, various preventive detention statutes (NSA, UAPA, BNSS), and significant judicial precedents like A.K. Gopalan.
Discusses the legal status of personality rights in India and the statutory framework for phone tapping (Telegraph Act, Post Office Act).
Covers the PUCL v. Union of India safeguards for tapping and the constitutional validity of narco-tests based on the Selvi judgment.

pages.subtopic: Article 21, ST Reservation, and Enemy Property
pages.rationale: Includes the Supreme Court's expansion of Article 21 to digital access, ST reservation in Goa, and the legal definition of enemy property.
Focuses primarily on Article 311 regarding the dismissal or removal of civil servants; lower density compared to previous pages.

pages.subtopic: Chief Justice of India and Appointment of Judges
pages.rationale: High-value content regarding the CJI's office, RTI status, and the evolution of the Collegium system through the Three Judges Cases.

pages.subtopic: Collegium System and Removal of Judges
pages.rationale: Explains the specific steps for HC/SC judge appointments and the constitutional/statutory process for the removal of judges.

pages.subtopic: Additional Judges, District Judges, and Tribunals
pages.rationale: Covers Article 224 (Additional Judges), Article 233 (District Judges), and the constitutional basis for Administrative Tribunals (323A/323B).
Details Article 39A, the Legal Services Authorities Act 1987, and the structure/functions of NALSA, SLSA, and DLSA.

pages.subtopic: Eligibility for Legal Aid and ADR Frameworks
pages.rationale: Lists specific categories eligible for free legal aid and provides a comprehensive guide to types of Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) and related acts.

pages.subtopic: Arbitral Awards, Lok Adalat, and Doctrine of Merger
pages.rationale: This page contains dense legal information regarding the Supreme Court's stance on modifying arbitral awards, the statutory status and powers of Lok Adalats, and the conceptual 'Doctrine of Merger'. These are high-yield topics for UPSC Prelims.
Focuses on the e-Zero FIR initiative and the legal basis for Zero FIR under the Bharatiya Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita. While specific, it is less dense than the preceding page.

pages.subtopic: Election Commission of India (ECI) and RUPPs
pages.rationale: Covers the constitutional and statutory framework of the ECI, recent electoral reforms, and judicial pronouncements. It also introduces Registered Unrecognized Political Parties (RUPP), providing multiple factual points.

pages.subtopic: Political Parties, Electoral Rolls, and CEC Appointment
pages.rationale: Provides a comparative table between Registered and Recognized parties, details on Special Intensive Revision, and the process for the appointment/removal of the CEC. High factual density.

pages.subtopic: CEC and EC Act 2023, ECINET, and Postal Ballot
pages.rationale: Extremely dense page detailing the 2023 Act for CEC/EC appointments (Search/Select Committees), the ECINET digital platform, and the start of the Postal Ballot section. Highly relevant for current affairs.
Detailed breakdown of eligibility for postal ballots (Service, Special, Absentee voters). Good for statement-based questions on electoral procedures.
Covers constitutional provisions of federalism (Articles 246-254), various coordination bodies (Inter-State Council, Zonal Councils), and the procedure for forming new states under Article 3.
Very high density page covering the Berubari Union case, constitutional articles for fiscal devolution (269-293), 15th Finance Commission recommendations, and the role of the Finance Commission.

pages.subtopic: State Revenues and Inter-State Water Disputes (ISWD)
pages.rationale: Lists sources of state revenue and the constitutional/statutory framework for resolving water disputes (Article 262, Entry 56/17).
Discusses the 2019 Amendment Bill and the River Basin Management Bill. The page contains an advertisement at the bottom, reducing the usable content area.
Comprehensive coverage of the DPDP Act 2023, including applicability, consent, the Data Protection Board, and obligations of fiduciaries. Highly probable for Prelims.

pages.subtopic: DPDP Rules 2025 and Criminal Law Reforms
pages.rationale: Covers the newly notified DPDP Rules 2025 and begins the section on the three new criminal laws (BNS 2023). High conceptual and factual density.

pages.subtopic: BNS, BNSS, BSA 2023 and Online Gaming Act 2025
pages.rationale: Dense comparison and key highlights of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita, and Sakshya Adhiniyam, plus the new Online Gaming Act.

pages.subtopic: Online Gaming Regulations and National Sports Governance Act
pages.rationale: Details the classification of online games, penalties, and the introduction of the National Sports Governance Act 2025.

pages.subtopic: National Sports Governance Framework and Tribunal
pages.rationale: Detailed provisions on the National Sports Board, National Sports Tribunal (composition and jurisdiction), and election oversight. Very specific and dense.
Explains the structure of National Sports Bodies and begins a detailed review of 5 years of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, specifically school education.
High-density page listing specific targets of NEP 2020 (GER, investment), related schemes (NIPUN Bharat, PM VidyaLakshmi), and the QS World University Rankings.
Covers the 20-year milestone of the Domestic Violence Act (provisions and institutional framework) and a significant Supreme Court expansion of the POSH Act ambit.
This page contains detailed provisions of the PoSH Act 2013, including definitions of sexual harassment, workplace, and the composition/powers of ICC and LCC, which are high-yield for UPSC Prelims.
Covers the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act 2010, its 2020 amendments, and rehabilitation schemes like Swadhar Greh and Ujjwala, providing specific regulatory and institutional facts.
Discusses the Waqf (Amendment) Act 2025, the UMEED portal, and the significant constitutional 'Doctrine of Essentiality' formulated by the Supreme Court.
Extremely dense page covering the framework of the Indian Census, including constitutional mandates (Art 246), the Census Act 1948, historical context, and new digital features for 2027.
Detailed features of Aadhaar, the statutory nature of UIDAI, and the landmark Justice K.S. Puttaswamy judgment regarding its constitutionality.
High factual density regarding OCI cardholder eligibility, restrictions, and revocation grounds, alongside the new Immigration and Foreigners (Exemption) Order 2025.
Covers the consolidation of immigration laws into the 2025 Act and the legal nuances of the land registration system in India, including recent Supreme Court observations.
Explains different land registration systems (Deeds vs Title), digital initiatives like ULPIN and NGDRS, and the 5-year progress of the SVAMITVA scheme.
Provides a detailed breakdown of Article 342, the Sixth Schedule (ADCs/ARCs), and the Fifth Schedule, including the Samatha judgment.
Compares the Fifth and Sixth Schedules and lists major tribal development initiatives like PM-JANMAN and the Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan.
Very dense page detailing the Tribal Village Vision 2030, the Adi Karmayogi Abhiyan, and the specific rights (land, MFP, grazing) under the Forest Rights Act 2006.
Covers the implementation of FRA, the Niyamgiri case, identification criteria for PVTGs, and the TALASH initiative for Eklavya Model Residential Schools.
Details the legal basis of the juvenile justice system in India, including definitions of children in conflict with law and the role of JJBs and NCPCR.
Compares the 2015 and 2021 JJ Acts and provides details on child adoption laws in India, including the role of CARA and the Hague Convention.

pages.subtopic: Online Content Regulation & Global Gender Gap
pages.rationale: Discusses the regulatory framework for OTT/online content (IT Act 2000, IT Rules 2021) and introduces the Global Gender Gap Report 2025 by WEF.

pages.subtopic: Global Gender Gap & Global Hunger Index 2025
pages.rationale: High-density data page covering India's performance across key dimensions of the Gender Gap Index and the composition/findings of the Global Hunger Index 2025.
Covers the SOFI 2025 report, UNICEF's Child Nutrition Report, and the detailed funding/implementation structure of Mission POSHAN 2.0.
Details the Maternity Benefit Act (1961/2017), relevant Supreme Court judgments on reproductive rights, and the ULLAS - New India Literacy Programme.
Contains detailed literacy statistics from PLFS 2023-24, digital literacy milestones in Kerala, and Swachh Survekshan 2024-25 awards.
Covers Swachh Shehar Jodi initiative, Kashi Declaration 2025 for drug-free India, and global drug control institutions like CND and UNODC.
High density of facts regarding the NDPS Act, WHO Global Tobacco Epidemic report 2025, MPOWER measures, and tobacco cultivation in India.
Discusses UN mechanisms for international AI governance and the Vishakhapatnam Declaration on digital competencies in civil services.
Provides detailed findings from the Performance Grade Index (PGI) 2.0 and the UDISE+ 2024-25 report on school education.
Covers the MERITE scheme for technical education, WHO's Age-Friendly Cities network, and the Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) data.
Details the diseases covered under the Universal Immunization Programme and the legal/constitutional framework for stray dog management.
A high-value comparative analysis of the powers, appointment, removal, and discretionary functions of the President and Governor.
Compares NRI, PIO, and OCI statuses and outlines the early historical development of civil services starting from the Cornwallis Code.

pages.subtopic: Civil Services History and Vital Statistics
pages.rationale: Traces the evolution of UPSC from 1853 to 1950 and provides key demographic trends from the SRS 2021 report.
Contains vital health statistics like U5MR, TFR, and Sex Ratio at Birth, alongside details on sports regulatory bodies like SAI and IOA.
Summarizes key achievements of NEP 2020 and provides a comprehensive list of major international reports and their releasing agencies.
Promotional content regarding Vision IAS results and upcoming foundation courses.
Promotional page featuring topper lists and contact information for various Vision IAS centers.
